description: Content Store — SHA-512 hashing utilities
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/store/ directory with mod.rs, hash.rs, layout.rs, error.rs
Implement SHA-512 hashing of file content using the sha2 crate
Return hex-encoded hash strings
Add unit tests verifying correct hash output for known inputs
Ensure no use of unwrap/expect — use proper Result error handling

description: Content Store — store directory layout with 2-char prefix sharding
steps: Implement store_path layout: ~/.aipm/store/{first-2-chars}/{remaining-chars}
Implement ContentStore struct with store_path field
Implement store_file() that hashes content and writes to correct path
Implement get_path() to retrieve file path by hash
Implement has_content() to check existence by hash
Use create_dir_all for directory creation (never create_dir)
Add unit tests with tempdir verifying storage and retrieval

description: Content Store — hard-link with cross-volume copy fallback
steps: Implement link_to() method on ContentStore
Use std::fs::hard_link to create hard links from store to target
Detect io::ErrorKind::CrossesDevices (EXDEV) and fall back to std::fs::copy
Emit warning via tracing::warn on fallback
Add unit tests verifying hard-link creation and fallback behavior

description: Content Store — store_package for extracting archive files into store
steps: Implement store_package() that walks an extracted directory
Hash each file individually and store in content-addressable store
Return BTreeMap<PathBuf, String> mapping relative paths to content hashes
Add unit tests with tempdir containing multiple files

description: Content Store — fs2 file locking for concurrent access
steps: Add fs2 advisory file locking on ~/.aipm/store/.lock
Acquire exclusive lock for store-wide write operations
Release lock on drop
Add unit tests verifying lock acquisition

description: Lockfile — types and TOML serialization/deserialization
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/lockfile/ directory with mod.rs, types.rs, reconcile.rs, error.rs
Define Lockfile struct with metadata and packages fields
Define LockfileMetadata with lockfile_version (u32) and generated_by (String)
Define LockedPackage with name, version, source, checksum, dependencies
Implement Serialize/Deserialize for all types
Add unit tests for round-trip serialization of lockfile TOML

description: Lockfile — read and write aipm.lock files
steps: Implement read_lockfile() to parse aipm.lock TOML from disk
Implement write_lockfile() to serialize and write aipm.lock to disk
Include header comment in written lockfile
Validate lockfile_version on read
Add unit tests for read/write round-trip with tempdir

description: Lockfile — drift detection for --locked mode
steps: Implement validate_lockfile_matches_manifest() function
Compare manifest dependency set against lockfile package set
Return error with details on any mismatch (added, removed, changed deps)
Add unit tests for matching and mismatching cases

description: Lockfile — minimal reconciliation algorithm
steps: Implement reconcile() in reconcile.rs
Diff manifest deps against lockfile: identify added, removed, changed deps
Prune removed deps and their exclusive transitive dependencies from lockfile
Return list of deps that need re-resolution (added/changed)
Carry forward existing pinned deps untouched
Add unit tests for each reconciliation scenario

description: Registry — trait definition and types
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/registry/ directory with mod.rs, git.rs, config.rs, error.rs
Define VersionEntry struct (name, vers, deps, cksum, features, yanked)
Define DepEntry struct (name, req, features, optional, default_features)
Define PackageMetadata struct (name, versions)
Define trait Registry with get_metadata() and download() methods
Define RegistryError enum with appropriate variants
Add unit tests for type serialization/deserialization

description: Registry — Git index repository layout and parsing
steps: Implement index file path calculation using 2-char prefix sharding scheme
Handle 1-char, 2-char, 3-char, and 4+ char package names
Handle scoped packages (@scope/name) with @sc/op/ prefix
Parse JSON-lines format from index files into Vec<VersionEntry>
Add unit tests for path calculation and JSON parsing

description: Registry — GitRegistry implementation with git2 cloning/fetching
steps: Implement GitRegistry struct with index cache at ~/.aipm/registry-cache/{hash}/
Clone index repo on first access using git2
Fetch updates on subsequent accesses using git2
Implement get_metadata() reading index file for the package
Parse config.json for dl URL template
Add integration tests with a test git repo

description: Registry — tarball download via reqwest with checksum verification
steps: Implement download() method on GitRegistry
Substitute {name} and {version} into dl URL template from config.json
Download tarball using reqwest::blocking::get
Verify SHA-512 checksum against index entry cksum field
Return error on checksum mismatch
Add unit tests with mock HTTP responses

description: Registry — configuration parsing for multiple registries and scope routing
steps: Parse [registries.default] and named registries from aipm.toml or ~/.aipm/config.toml
Parse [registries.scopes] for scope-to-registry mapping
Route scoped packages (@scope/name) to correct registry
Default all unscoped packages to 'default' registry
Add unit tests for config parsing and scope routing

description: Resolver — backtracking constraint solver core loop
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/resolver/ directory with mod.rs, graph.rs, solver.rs, features.rs, overrides.rs, error.rs
Implement resolve() function taking root deps, registry, and lockfile pins
Seed activated set with lockfile pins for carried-forward deps
Try candidates from highest to lowest version
Filter out yanked versions (unless pinned in lockfile)
Implement backtracking on conflict
Return Resolution with all resolved packages
Add unit tests with mock Registry for simple dependency chains

description: Resolver — version unification and cross-major coexistence
steps: Implement version unification: if existing activated version satisfies new requirement, reuse it
Implement cross-major coexistence: different major versions can coexist
Implement same-major conflict detection triggering backtrack
Add unit tests for unification, coexistence, and conflict scenarios
Cover BDD scenarios from resolution.feature (13 P0 scenarios)

description: Resolver — override application (global, scoped, replacement)
steps: Implement apply_overrides() before resolution begins
Handle global overrides: replace version requirement everywhere
Handle scoped overrides (parent>child): replace only under named parent
Handle replacement overrides (aipm:other@version): swap package name entirely
Add unit tests for each override type

description: Resolver — features system with additive unification
steps: Track feature sets per activated package in resolver
Enable default feature unless consumer sets default-features = false
Union features from all consumers of a package
Handle dep: syntax for feature-conditional optional dependencies
Re-evaluate when new features activate optional deps requiring additional resolution
Add unit tests covering BDD scenarios from features.feature (6 P1 scenarios)

description: Resolver — detailed conflict error reporting
steps: Define ConflictError with fields for conflicting package, both incompatible requirements, and dependency chain
Produce human-readable error message naming the conflict
Include the path from root to each conflicting requirement
Add unit tests verifying error output format

description: Linker — symlink (Unix) and directory junction (Windows) creation
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/linker/ directory with mod.rs, directory_link.rs, hard_link.rs, gitignore.rs, link_state.rs, error.rs
Implement create_directory_link() using std::os::unix::fs::symlink on Unix
Implement create_directory_link() using junction::create on Windows via cfg
Add unit tests for link creation and verification

description: Linker — hard-link assembly from store to .aipm/links/
steps: Implement assemble_package() to create .aipm/links/{pkg}/ directory
Hard-link each file from content store to assembled directory
Preserve directory structure from original package
Fall back to copy on cross-volume error
Add unit tests with tempdir

description: Linker — three-tier linking pipeline (store → .aipm/links → claude-plugins)
steps: Implement link_package() orchestrating the full three-tier link
Hard-link from store to .aipm/links/{pkg}/
Create directory symlink/junction from claude-plugins/{pkg}/ to .aipm/links/{pkg}/
Create parent directories as needed with create_dir_all
Add unit tests verifying complete link chain

description: Linker — gitignore managed section read/write
steps: Implement add_gitignore_entry() to insert package name between aipm markers
Implement remove_gitignore_entry() to remove from managed section
Create .gitignore file with markers if it doesn't exist
Preserve manual entries outside the managed markers
For scoped packages, add both @scope/name and @scope/ entries
Add unit tests for add, remove, and preserve scenarios

description: Linker — link state tracking via .aipm/links.toml
steps: Implement read/write for .aipm/links.toml tracking active dev link overrides
Store name, path, linked_at for each link entry
Add entry on aipm link, remove on aipm unlink
Clear all entries on aipm install --locked
Implement list function for aipm list --linked
Add unit tests for state management

description: Filesystem trait extensions for install/link operations
steps: Extend existing Fs trait in crates/libaipm/src/fs.rs with new methods
Add remove_file(), remove_dir_all(), hard_link(), copy_file()
Add symlink_dir(), read_link(), is_symlink(), atomic_write()
Implement for RealFs (production) and MockFs (testing)
Add unit tests for each new method

description: Installer — orchestrate resolve → fetch → store → link → lockfile pipeline
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/installer/ directory with mod.rs, pipeline.rs, manifest_editor.rs, error.rs
Implement install() function orchestrating the full pipeline
Step 1: Load manifest and lockfile
Step 2: If --locked, validate lockfile matches manifest
Step 3: If adding a new package, add to manifest via toml_edit
Step 4: Resolve dependencies (lockfile-first, reconciliation, or full)
Step 5: Fetch .aipm tarballs not in global store, verify checksums
Step 6: Extract files into global store by content hash
Step 7: Assemble .aipm/links/{pkg}/ with hard-links
Step 8: Create directory links into plugins dir + update .gitignore
Step 9: If --locked, remove all dev link overrides with warnings
Step 10: Write aipm.lock with exact versions + checksums
Add integration tests covering BDD scenarios from install.feature and lockfile.feature

description: Installer — manifest editing with toml_edit for comment preservation
steps: Implement add_dependency_to_manifest() using toml_edit crate
Parse aipm.toml preserving comments and formatting
Add new dependency entry under [dependencies] table
Handle version spec parsing (e.g., @^1.0 syntax)
Write modified manifest back preserving existing formatting
Add unit tests verifying comment preservation

description: Installer — update command for re-resolving to latest compatible versions
steps: Implement update() function in installer module
If package specified, only re-resolve that dependency
If no package specified, re-resolve all dependencies
Fetch new versions from registry
Update lockfile with new resolution
Re-link updated packages
Add unit tests for targeted and full update scenarios

description: CLI — aipm install command with --locked flag
steps: Add InstallArgs struct with package (Option<String>), locked (bool), registry (Option<String>)
Wire install subcommand into aipm CLI via clap
Parse package@version syntax from positional argument
Call installer::install() with appropriate parameters
Handle and display errors from installer pipeline
Add integration tests using assert_cmd + tempfile

description: CLI — aipm update command
steps: Add UpdateArgs struct with optional package name
Wire update subcommand into aipm CLI via clap
Call installer::update() with appropriate parameters
Handle and display errors
Add integration tests

description: CLI — aipm link and aipm unlink commands
steps: Add LinkArgs struct with path (PathBuf)
Add UnlinkArgs struct with package (String)
Wire link and unlink subcommands into aipm CLI
aipm link: validate path contains aipm.toml, read package name, create symlink, update links.toml
aipm unlink: remove symlink, restore registry link, update links.toml
Add integration tests covering BDD scenarios from link.feature

description: CLI — aipm list --linked command
steps: Add list subcommand with --linked flag
Read .aipm/links.toml and display active link overrides
Show package name, linked path, and linked_at timestamp
Add integration tests

description: Register new modules in libaipm lib.rs
steps: Add pub mod store, lockfile, registry, resolver, linker, installer to lib.rs
Ensure all public APIs are accessible from the library root
Verify workspace builds cleanly with cargo build --workspace

description: Add new workspace dependencies to Cargo.toml
steps: Add git2 0.19 to workspace dependencies
Move reqwest 0.12 (blocking, json, rustls-tls) to libaipm dependencies
Add toml_edit 0.22 to libaipm dependencies
Add fs2 0.4 to libaipm dependencies
Verify cargo build --workspace succeeds with new deps

description: Security — lifecycle script blocking by default
steps: Block lifecycle script execution during install by default
Require [install].allowed_build_scripts allowlist for any script execution
Log warning when scripts are found but not in allowlist
Add unit tests verifying scripts are blocked without allowlist

description: Observability — structured logging with tracing for install pipeline
steps: Add tracing instrumentation to all install pipeline operations
Log fetch, extraction, linking, and lockfile operations
Support --verbose flag for debug-level output
Add resolver conflict detailed error reports naming conflicting package and incompatible requirements
